Title: B9. Polymer Adiabatic Force Law

Node started: Technical Specification: Derivation of the Adiabatic Force Law for a Freely Jointed Chain. Objective: Derive the exact analytical expression for the force F(x) exerted by a freely jointed polymer chain of n mass points (connected by n struts of length l) as a function of end-to-end distance x, under the constraint of thermal isolation (adiabatic process). Physical System Definition: 1. Model: A freely jointed chain (FJC) consisting of n rigid segments of length l. 2. Degrees of Freedom: The configuration is defined by the orientation of each segment. 3. Thermodynamic Constraint: The system is thermally isolated. The derivation must account for the change in internal energy/entropy such that the process follows an adiabatic path (dQ = 0), rather than an isothermal path. Analytical Requirements & Derivation Steps: 1. Partition Function Derivation (Isothermal Baseline): Start with the canonical partition function Z(x, T) for a freely jointed chain at constant temperature T. Utilize the central limit theorem approximation for large n to express the probability distribution of the end-to-end distance x. Relate Z(x, T) to the Helmholtz free energy A(x, T) = -kB T ln Z(x, T). 2. Equation of State (Isothermal Force): Derive the isothermal force F_iso(x, T) = (dA/dx)_T. For the FJC in the linear regime (small x/nl), identify the relationship F_iso proportional to x. 3. Adiabatic Constraint Application: Define the adiabatic condition using the First Law of Thermodynamics: dU = dQ - dW. For an adiabatic process, dU = -F dx. Use the relation between internal energy U, entropy S, and temperature T: dU = T dS - P dV (or in this context, dU = T dS - F dx). Apply the adiabatic condition dS = (1/T) dU or use the relation for the adiabatic exponent/index if applicable to the specific degrees of freedom. Crucial Step: Determine how the temperature T evolves as a function of x during the adiabatic compression/extension to maintain dS = 0. 4. Force Law Synthesis: Combine the temperature dependence T(x) with the isothermal force expression to find the total force F_adia(x) = (dU/dx)_S. Alternatively, use the relation F_adia(x) = F_iso(x, T) + T (dS/dx)_T and solve for the adiabatic path. Target Output Format: FINAL_ANSWER: An exact formula for F(x).
